$comment: TraigentSchema#132: governance registry of allowed JSON-Schema x-* extension keywords. Every x-* key used anywhere under traigent_schema/schemas/ MUST be registered here (enforced by tests/test_x_extension_governance.py). Adding a new x-* keyword to a schema requires adding it here with a one-line description — turning ad-hoc x-* sprawl into a documented, reviewable vocabulary.

x-content.description: Marks a leaf field as carrying user content; pairs with x-privacy-classification. (#78)

x-privacy-classification.description: Privacy class of a field's value, e.g. "user_content". (#78)

x-reference.description: Documents that a field references another resource/entity (informational FK note).

x-references.description: Block of cross-references to related schemas / backend evidence (e.g. RBAC vocabulary).

x-identifier.description: Marks a field as an identifier of the enclosing resource.

x-source-of-truth.description: Names the canonical vocabulary a schema owns (e.g. api_key_authorization_vocabulary).

x-canonical-unit.description: The canonical unit for a vocabulary's values (e.g. millisecond for timing metrics). (#118)

x-naming-convention.description: Documents the naming/suffix convention for a vocabulary's keys.

x-canonical-timing-metrics.description: Map of canonical timing-metric keys to unit/role/aggregate/scope. (#118)

x-legacy-timing-aliases.description: Map of legacy timing-metric aliases to their canonical key + conversion factor. (#118)

x-non-canonical-pending-migration.description: Timing keys emitted today but pending migration to a canonical key. (#118)

x-timing-metric-vocabulary.description: Non-constraining pointer from a payload schema to the timing-metric vocabulary. (#118)

x-privilege-roles.description: RBAC privilege roles (member/admin) and whether each is privileged. (#144)

x-privileged-scopes.description: API-key scopes that grant privileged/admin access.

x-scoping-rules.description: Per-rule request-scoping policy (owner filter, tenant scope, project membership). (#144)

x-admin-bypass-summary.description: Human-readable summary of which controls admins bypass. (#144)

x-permission-convention.description: Token convention for canonical backend permissions.

x-scope-permission-map.description: Bridge mapping API-key scopes to backend permissions + compatibility aliases.

x-user-requestable-scopes.description: API-key scopes an ordinary user may request.

x-required-permission.description: Permission token required to call an endpoint/operation.

x-required-permission-vocabulary.description: Pointer from an endpoints catalog to the permission vocabulary.

x-canonical-permission.description: The canonical permission token for a scope/alias.

x-endpoint-modules.description: Master-catalog list of endpoint modules (paths_file references).

x-response-headers.description: Documents response headers for an operation.

x-traigent-body.description: Marks the canonical request/response body schema for a Traigent operation.

x-traigent-contract.description: Names the Traigent contract a schema/operation belongs to.

x-traigent-optimization-capabilities.description: Declares optimization capabilities exposed by an operation.

x-wrap-map.description: Success-envelope wrapping map (which payload key wraps the resource).
